It appears to be that a plant called Sassafras latifolia doesn't exist but another plant that shares a similar name is Sassafras albidum. Sassafras albidum is a plant in the family Lauraceae, native to Eastern North America, commonly referred to as Sassafras.

Sassafras albidum is defined as a plant that is a deciduous tree or shrub, growing up to 35 meters in height, with aromatic roots and leaves.

Sassafras albidum has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.

The bioactive constituents of Sassafras albidum include safrole, a compound that has been shown to exhibit antiparasitic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other compounds like myristicin, which has been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.

Sassafras albidum has been used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
